title: pittosporum parviflorum
intro: Pittosporum parviflorum, also known as the Tui Tree or Kohuhu, is a small to medium-sized tree native to New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Its health benefits are attributed to its ability to aid in digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and berries can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although caution and proper dosage should be exercised due to potential toxicity.
